chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: whenever we want to render an array, for example if we are writing an array

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: for example if I have written my array like this

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so what is happening here is that all the values are being printed for us

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: but we want to display all the values in the form of elements or a list

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so in that case we have to convert each individual array value into a separate item

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: and we have two ways to do that

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: the first way is that when we are sending an array, we are only sending values for now

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: that means this is an individual value which we have sent

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: this is an individual value which we have not sent

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so instead of sending individual values, we should send an array of elements

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: for example what does it mean to say elements

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: for now let's remove this and pass our options array here

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so for now all the individual values are being sent but what can we do

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: we can send an array of elements, an array of HTML elements

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: how? we can make an li element here

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: in which we can write our text

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: and this li element will become a part of our array

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: it will become a value kept on the index of our array

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so when we write code in react, it is possible to pass our values in the array in this way

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: we can create another li element here

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: let's remove this

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: and write it here

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: and we will get another li element

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: in which this thing will come

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so now when we directly pass our options array

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so not only a string will go inside it, a complete HTML element will go inside it

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: and when we will print our features inside this paragraph

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so what JSX will do? JSX will take out one element of the array

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: what is in one element of the array? it is a complete list item

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so when the list will be printed, the first list item will be printed

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: then the second list item will be printed

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: then the third list item will be printed

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so in this way when we want our values of the array to be wrapped inside an element

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so we can send them by pre-wrapping them inside the element

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: there is another way to do this which we will use more frequently

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: which is we have sent simple values from our product tab

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: the values that are going from the product tab are our simple strings

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: but when values are coming inside the product

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: when we are printing the feature

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so here we can map things for the entire array

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: the work of map function is to take every element of the array

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: and make some changes inside it

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so we want to wrap the normal string element of the array inside an li

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: and we want to repeat this for every element of the array

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so for that we can use the map method

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: how will we use it?

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: here we will make a new variable

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: let's call it list

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: and what will happen with list?

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: we will map our features array

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: by mapping it we will take our individual feature

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: we can also write it as element

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: this is not features, this is feature

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: we will convert every individual feature to this li

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: inside which our feature value will come separately

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so every feature value which was original will be replaced to this element

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: which has li and li has feature value

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: and this time when we have to render

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so we will be rendering our list directly

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: rather than the features array

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: we will save it and refresh it

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so we will get the same output

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: one thing you might have noticed

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: that the first thing we talked about is that

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: that wheat is faster and quicker

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: you can notice that change here

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: because when we use nodemon or nodejs

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so we don't see that much instant change

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: but here inside the UI itself

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: you can see the changes are very fast

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: now if we want we can copy this code directly here

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: so generally what we do is

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3
chunks.text: we write the whole logic here

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3

chunks.title: Rendering Arrays.mp3

JSX will take out one element of the array what is in one element of the array? it is a complete list item so when the list will be printed, the first list item will be printed then the second list item will be printed then the third list item will be printed so in this way when we want our values of the array to be wrapped inside an element so we can send them by pre-wrapping them inside the element there is another way to do this which we will use more frequently which is we have sent simple values from our product tab the values that are going from the product tab are our simple strings but when values are coming inside the product when we are printing the feature so here we can map things for the entire array the work of map function is to take every element of the array and make some changes inside it so we want to wrap the normal string element of the array inside an li and we want to repeat this for every element of the array so for that we can use the map method how will we use it? here we will make a new variable let's call it list and what will happen with list? we will map our features array by mapping it we will take our individual feature we can also write it as element this is not features, this is feature we will convert every individual feature to this li inside which our feature value will come separately so every feature value which was original will be replaced to this element which has li and li has feature value and this time when we have to render so we will be rendering our list directly rather than the features array we will save it and refresh it so we will get the same output one thing you might have noticed that the first thing we talked about is that that wheat is faster and quicker you can notice that change here because when we use nodemon or nodejs so we don't see that much instant change but here inside the UI itself you can see the changes are very fast now if we want we can copy this code directly here so generally what we do is we write the whole logic here rather than making a separate variable we save it and refresh it so we will get the same output now map method can be done some students might have missed out on revision time so revise the map method once because in react whenever we have to render the array so we will be using map method very frequently
